# First-Half 2026 Almanac Briefing for a Newsroom
## Defining events of January–July 2026 across sports, entertainment and space

This briefing covers five anchor stories that defined the first half of 2026: the expanded men’s FIFA World Cup now underway in North America; Super Bowl LX; the 2026 NBA Finals; the 98th Academy Awards; and NASA’s Artemis II lunar mission. All claims below are grounded in cited reporting or official sources.

---

## 1) 2026 FIFA World Cup so far

The 2026 FIFA World Cup is the first men’s World Cup with **48 teams**, expanded from 32, and the first co-hosted by **three countries: Canada, Mexico and the United States**. FIFA says the tournament runs from **June 11 to July 19, 2026**, features **12 groups of four**, and sends the **top two teams in each group plus the eight best third-place teams** into a new **Round of 32**, producing a **104-match** tournament.  
Sources: FIFA format explainer: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/article-fifa-world-cup-2026-mexico-canada-usa-new-format-tournament-football-soccer ; FIFA schedule page: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/match-schedule-fixtures-results-teams-stadiums

FIFA identifies **16 host cities** across the three countries. It says the tournament opened at **Mexico City Stadium (Estadio Azteca)** and will end with the **final at New York New Jersey Stadium**.  
Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/fifa-world-cup-2026-hosts-cities-dates-usa-mexico-canada ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/watch-host-cities-2026-world-cup-video

### Where the tournament stands on July 4
By July 4, FIFA says the **group stage has ended** and the tournament has advanced through the **Round of 32** into the **Round of 16**.  
Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket

### Knockout rounds so far: results and major turns
The completed Round-of-32 results include:

- **Canada 1, South Africa 0**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/17/285023/289287/400021519
- **Brazil 2, Japan 1**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/brazil-paraguay-round-32-review-highlights ; https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket
- **Paraguay 1, Germany 1 (Paraguay won 4-3 on penalties)**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/17/285023/289287/400021513 ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/brazil-paraguay-round-32-review-highlights
- **Morocco 2, Netherlands 2 (Morocco won 3-2 on penalties)**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **France 3, Sweden 0**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026 ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/teams/france/fixtures
- **Mexico 2, Ecuador 0**  
  Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **England 2, Congo DR 1**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/england-congo-dr-review-highlights ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/bosnia-england-belgium-usa-review-highlights
- **Belgium 3, Senegal 2**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/belgium-comeback-senegal ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/bosnia-england-belgium-usa-review-highlights
- **United States 2, Bosnia and Herzegovina 0**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/usa-bosnia-and-herzegovina-review-highlights ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/bosnia-england-belgium-usa-review-highlights
- **Switzerland 2, Algeria 0**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/slick-switzerland-sweep-past-algeria-into-world-cup-last-16-2026-07-03/
- **Spain 3, Austria 0**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **Portugal 2, Croatia 1**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **Argentina 3, Cabo Verde 2 (after extra time)**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/matchday-23-round-up-review-highlights ; https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/argentina-made-suffer-cape-verde-world-cup-scare-says-scaloni-2026-07-04/
- **Egypt 0, Australia 0 (Egypt won 4-2 on penalties)**  
  Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/matchday-23-round-up-review-highlights
- **Colombia 1, Ghana 0**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026 ; https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/lorenzo-praises-colombias-defensive-resolve-after-win-over-ghana-2026-07-04/

### Biggest upsets and drama
Several results have already shaped the tournament narrative:

- **Paraguay knocking out Germany on penalties** stands out as a major upset.  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/17/285023/289287/400021513 ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/brazil-paraguay-round-32-review-highlights
- **Morocco eliminating the Netherlands on penalties** is another bracket-shaking result.  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **Cabo Verde nearly stunned Argentina**, forcing the defending champion to extra time before losing 3-2. Reuters described Argentina as “made to suffer,” while FIFA called it a “scare.”  
  Sources: https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/argentina-made-suffer-cape-verde-world-cup-scare-says-scaloni-2026-07-04/ ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/matchday-23-round-up-review-highlights
- **Belgium rallied from 2-0 down to beat Senegal 3-2**, one of the knockout stage’s most dramatic comebacks.  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/belgium-comeback-senegal ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/bosnia-england-belgium-usa-review-highlights
- The **United States advanced despite playing much of the second half with 10 men** after Folarin Balogun’s red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina.  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/usa-bosnia-and-herzegovina-review-highlights ; https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/us-coach-pochettino-says-balogun-did-not-deserve-red-card-2026-07-02/

### Upcoming Round-of-16 schedule / pairings
As of July 4, the Round of 16 bracket includes:

- **Portugal vs. Spain**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **United States vs. Belgium**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/usa-belgium-live-stream-team-news-tickets-and-more ; https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket
- **Brazil vs. Norway**  
  Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/scores-fixtures
- **Mexico vs. England**  
  Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026
- **Paraguay vs. France**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026 ; https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/frances-tchouameni-miss-paraguay-clash-with-thigh-injury-says-report-2026-07-04/
- **Canada vs. Morocco**  
  Sources: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026 ; https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/canada-morocco-live-stream-team-news-tickets
- **Argentina vs. Egypt**  
  Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/matchday-23-round-up-review-highlights
- **Switzerland vs. Colombia**  
  Sources: https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/slick-switzerland-sweep-past-algeria-into-world-cup-last-16-2026-07-03/ ; https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/lorenzo-praises-colombias-defensive-resolve-after-win-over-ghana-2026-07-04/ ; https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket

FIFA’s live fixtures page listed **Brazil vs. Norway on July 4 at New York/New Jersey Stadium** as a Round-of-16 match, underlining that the tournament had shifted from bracket formation to live Round-of-16 play.  
Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/scores-fixtures

**Newsroom takeaway:** The World Cup’s expansion has produced a denser, more chaotic knockout bracket, with traditional powers still alive but early volatility already visible in Germany’s exit, Morocco’s progress, and a near-shock for Argentina.

---

## 2) Super Bowl LX

**Super Bowl LX** was played on **February 8, 2026**, at **Levi’s Stadium in Santa Clara, California**. The **Seattle Seahawks beat the New England Patriots 29-13**, and **Kenneth Walker III** was named **Super Bowl MVP**.  
Sources: NFL Super Bowl hub: https://www.nfl.com/super-bowl ; NFL event page: https://www.nfl.com/news/official-lineup-of-super-bowl-lx-events-and-venues-revealed ; AP live coverage: https://apnews.com/live/super-bowl-60-2026-updates

The game carried historical weight beyond the scoreline. Reuters noted that New England’s appearance was its **record-extending 12th Super Bowl**, while AP reported that Seattle’s win delivered the franchise’s **second Super Bowl title**. The matchup also revived memories of **Super Bowl XLIX**, when the Patriots beat the Seahawks 28-24.  
Sources: https://www.reuters.com/sports/everything-you-need-know-about-2026-super-bowl-2026-01-26 ; https://apnews.com/live/super-bowl-60-2026-updates ; https://abcnews.go.com/GMA/Culture/super-bowl-2026-how-to-watch-seahawks-patriots/story?id=129667770

**Newsroom takeaway:** Super Bowl LX combined a high-recognition matchup with a clean, decisive Seattle win, giving the NFL year an early signature result.

---

## 3) The 2026 NBA Finals

The **2026 NBA Finals** matched the **New York Knicks** against the **San Antonio Spurs**, and New York won the series **4-1**.  
Sources: NBA Finals hub: https://www.nba.com/playoffs/2026/nba-finals ; NBA Finals schedule/news page: https://www.nba.com/news/2026-nba-finals-schedule

The Knicks clinched in **Game 5 on June 13, 2026**, beating the Spurs **94-90**. **Jalen Brunson** was named **NBA Finals MVP**.  
Sources: https://www.nba.com/game/nyk-vs-sas-0042500405 ; https://www.nba.com/news/jalen-brunson-wins-bill-russell-trophy-as-2026-nba-finals-mvp ; https://www.nba.com/watch/list/collection/2026-nba-finals-game-recap-videos

The title was historically resonant: NBA coverage states it was the **Knicks’ first championship since 1973**, ending a **53-year drought**. NBA coverage also said New York’s Game 4 win featured the **largest comeback in NBA Finals history**. Yahoo Sports, citing ESPN data, reported that the series became the **most-watched NBA Finals since 1998**.  
Sources: https://www.nba.com/news/2026-nba-finals-schedule ; https://www.nba.com/watch/list/collection/2026-nba-finals-game-recap-videos ; https://sports.yahoo.com/sports-business/article/2026-nba-finals-knicks-spurs-most-watched-finals-since-michael-jordan-capped-last-dance-with-bulls-in-1998-214810687.html

**Newsroom takeaway:** In pure cultural impact, the Knicks’ title may have been the biggest North American sports story of the spring—an iconic franchise finally ending one of the league’s longest championship droughts.

---

## 4) The 98th Academy Awards

The **98th Academy Awards** were held on **March 15, 2026**, with **Conan O’Brien** as host.  
Sources: https://www.oscars.org/oscars ; https://www.oscars.org/oscars/98th-oscars-and-academy-key-dates ; https://press.oscars.org/news/98th-oscarsr-rounds-out-show-presenters-rose-byrne-nicole-kidman-jimmy-kimmel-delroy-lindo

### Major winners
- **Best Picture:** *One Battle After Another*  
- **Best Director:** **Paul Thomas Anderson**, *One Battle After Another*  
- **Best Actor:** **Michael B. Jordan**, *Sinners*  
- **Best Actress:** **Jessie Buckley**, *Hamnet*  
- **Best Supporting Actor:** **Sean Penn**, *One Battle After Another*  
- **Best Supporting Actress:** **Amy Madigan**, *Weapons*  
- **Best Original Screenplay:** **Ryan Coogler**, *Sinners*  
- **Best Adapted Screenplay:** **Paul Thomas Anderson**, *One Battle After Another*  
- **Best International Feature Film:** *Sentimental Value* (Norway)  
- **Best Animated Feature Film:** *KPop Demon Hunters*  
- **Best Documentary Feature Film:** *Mr. Nobody Against Putin*  

Sources: Oscars ceremony page: https://www.oscars.org/oscars/ceremonies/2026 ; AP winners list: https://apnews.com/article/2026-oscars-winners-list-5d9f5670eda0440276377a5f6c8b60b9 ; PBS/AP roundup: https://www.pbs.org/newshour/arts/heres-a-full-list-of-2026-academy-awards-winners ; category stories: https://apnews.com/article/oscars-2026-best-international-film-ac9d608fec5a03ecbe2d0a0b67b44bbd ; https://apnews.com/article/best-animated-film-2026-oscars-dbb9e8d1a72f5552f3d7841519ab9b59 ; https://apnews.com/article/best-documentary-2026-oscars-bf4320316a8e98285debfd6c2ce8b551 ; Academy Museum doc winner listing: https://www.academymuseum.org/programs/detail/98th-oscars-documentary-feature-winner-019b0aab-2e79-494b-7ddb-a6981e3b9d9b

### Films that dominated
The ceremony had two dominant film narratives:

- *One Battle After Another* was the **biggest winner by trophies**, taking **6 Oscars**, including picture and director.  
  Sources: https://apnews.com/live/oscars-2026-updates ; https://apnews.com/article/2026-oscars-winners-list-5d9f5670eda0440276377a5f6c8b60b9
- *Sinners* entered the night as the **most nominated film**, with **16 nominations**, which AP described as a record, and won key awards including **Best Actor** and **Best Original Screenplay**.  
  Sources: https://apnews.com/article/oscar-nominations-2026-83798def8de7626b011aba3c043a4115 ; https://apnews.com/article/oscars-2026-what-to-know-3f8cbb0f7c9ede99bbe7efa2386f5b92

**Newsroom takeaway:** Oscar night split into a classic prestige picture storyline—*One Battle After Another* sweeping the top race—and a star/industry storyline around *Sinners* converting historic nomination strength into marquee wins.

---

## 5) NASA’s Artemis II

By July 4, **Artemis II had already flown and returned successfully**. NASA’s launch-day coverage says the mission lifted off on **April 1, 2026, at 6:35 p.m. EDT** from **Launch Complex 39B** at Kennedy Space Center.  
Source: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/01/live-artemis-ii-launch-day-updates

NASA describes Artemis II as the **first crewed mission of the Artemis program** and the agency’s **first crewed lunar flyby in 50 years**.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/mission/artemis-ii ; https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/01/live-artemis-ii-launch-day-updates

### Crew
The four astronauts were:

- **Reid Wiseman** — commander  
- **Victor Glover** — pilot  
- **Christina Koch** — mission specialist  
- **Jeremy Hansen** — mission specialist, Canadian Space Agency  

Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/feature/our-artemis-crew ; https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-names-astronauts-to-next-moon-mission-first-crew-under-artemis

### Mission profile, records and outcome
NASA says Artemis II was designed as an **approximately 10-day** mission around the Moon and back, validating the **Space Launch System**, **Orion**, and associated ground systems with crew aboard in deep space.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/missions/nasa-answers-your-most-pressing-artemis-ii-questions ; https://www.nasa.gov/mission/artemis-ii ; https://www.nasa.gov/feature/our-artemis-crew

During the mission:

- The crew flew around the **far side of the Moon on April 6, 2026**.  
- NASA said the mission surpassed the previous **farthest human spaceflight from Earth** record that day, later listing a maximum distance of **252,756 miles from Earth**.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/09/artemis-ii-flight-day-9-crew-prepares-to-come-home ; https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasas-artemis-ii-crew-eclipses-record-for-farthest-human-spaceflight ; https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/06/artemis-ii-flight-day-6-lunar-flyby-updates

NASA also notes the mission’s symbolic firsts: **Victor Glover** became the **first Black astronaut on a lunar mission**, **Christina Koch** the **first woman on a lunar mission**, and **Jeremy Hansen** the **first Canadian on a lunar mission**.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-names-astronauts-to-next-moon-mission-first-crew-under-artemis ; https://www.nasa.gov/feature/our-artemis-crew

The mission ended successfully when Orion **splashed down in the Pacific off California on April 10, 2026, at 8:07 p.m. EDT**. NASA later said its live coverage generated **more than 149.4 million views** across agency platforms, a new NASA streaming record.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/10/artemis-ii-flight-day-10-re-entry-live-updates ; https://www.nasa.gov/general/nasas-artemis-ii-breaks-agency-streaming-record

**Newsroom takeaway:** Artemis II was not just a successful mission but a major public event—an operational milestone, a record-setting human flight, and a symbolic reset in lunar exploration.

---

## Bottom line for editors

Across the first seven months of 2026, the biggest cross-desk stories were:
- a **radically expanded World Cup** already producing upsets and a crowded knockout picture in North America,
- a **Seattle Super Bowl win** over New England,
- a **Knicks championship** ending a 53-year drought,
- an Oscars split between ***One Battle After Another*** and ***Sinners***,
- and **Artemis II’s successful crewed lunar flyby**, NASA’s boldest human-spaceflight step in decades.

Short direct answer: The first half of 2026 was defined by an expanded World Cup entering a volatile knockout phase, Seattle winning Super Bowl LX, the Knicks winning their first NBA title since 1973, *One Battle After Another* leading the Oscars, and NASA successfully flying Artemis II around the Moon and back.

# Newsroom Almanac Briefing: The Defining Events of January–July 2026 in Sports, Entertainment and Space

## Executive summary

The first half of 2026 has already produced several durable headline events across sports, entertainment and space: the first 48-team men’s FIFA World Cup is now in its round of 16; the Seattle Seahawks won Super Bowl LX; the New York Knicks ended a 53-year title drought in the NBA Finals; the Oscars consolidated **_One Battle After Another_** as the year’s top awards film while **_Sinners_** also broke through in major categories; and NASA’s **Artemis II** became the first crewed lunar mission since 1972, setting a new human-distance record before splashing down safely in April. Sources are cited inline with URLs.

---

## 1) 2026 FIFA World Cup so far

### The big structural story: the first 48-team men’s World Cup

The 2026 tournament is the largest in men’s World Cup history. FIFA expanded the field from 32 to **48 teams**, with the new format producing **12 groups of four**, followed by a new **Round of 32**, then the round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals and final. The tournament runs from **11 June to 19 July 2026** and includes **104 matches**. FIFA says the event is jointly hosted by **Canada, Mexico and the United States**, the first men’s World Cup shared by three countries.  
Sources: FIFA format explainer: https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/article-fifa-world-cup-2026-mexico-canada-usa-new-format-tournament-football-soccer ; FIFA hosts/dates overview: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/fifa-world-cup-2026-hosts-cities-dates-usa-mexico-canada

### Hosts and marquee staging details

FIFA’s official hosts page identifies **Canada, Mexico and the USA** as co-hosts. FIFA also confirmed that **Mexico City Stadium** stages the opening match and **New York New Jersey Stadium** will host the final.  
Source: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/fifa-world-cup-2026-hosts-cities-dates-usa-mexico-canada

### Knockout rounds so far: what has happened

As of **4 July 2026**, the tournament has completed the new **Round of 32** and moved into the **round of 16**. FIFA’s knockout-stage tracker and match pages show a bracket full of heavyweights, but also several notable shocks.  
Source: FIFA knockout tracker: https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/knockout-stage-match-schedule-bracket

## 2) Super Bowl LX

### Result and core facts

**Super Bowl LX** was played on **8 February 2026** at **Levi’s Stadium** in **Santa Clara, California**. The **Seattle Seahawks** defeated the **New England Patriots**, **29-13**, to win the franchise’s **second** Super Bowl title.  
Sources: Levi’s Stadium event page: https://levisstadium.com/event/super-bowl-lx ; NFL coverage: https://www.nfl.com/news/seahawks-patriots-in-super-bowl-lx-what-we-learned-from-seattle-s-29-13-win ; Seahawks team recap: https://www.seahawks.com/video/final-minutes-of-the-super-bowl-lx-victory-vs-the-new-england-patriots-seattle-seahawks

### MVP

The NFL named **Kenneth Walker III** the **Super Bowl LX MVP** after he totaled **161 scrimmage yards**, including **135 rushing yards on 27 carries** and **two catches for 26 yards**. NFL.com notes that his 135 rushing yards were the most in a Super Bowl since Terrell Davis ran for 157 in Super Bowl XXXII.  
Source: https://www.nfl.com/news/seahawks-rb-kenneth-walker-iii-named-super-bowl-lx-mvp

### Game shape and notable detail

The game was controlled largely by Seattle’s defense and kicking game before opening up later. NFL.com says kicker **Jason Myers** made a **Super Bowl-record five field goals** and scored **17 points**, while Seattle’s defense repeatedly disrupted Patriots quarterback **Drake Maye**.  
Sources: https://www.nfl.com/news/seahawks-jason-myers-first-200-point-scorer-record-five-field-goals-super-bowl-lx ; https://www.nfl.com/news/seahawks-cb-devon-witherspoon-shines-brightest-for-seattle-s-defense-in-super-bowl-lx-win-over-patriots

### Significance

For Seattle, the win delivered the club’s **second Lombardi Trophy** and capped one of the more surprising quarterback redemption arcs in recent years with **Sam Darnold** at the helm.  
Sources: https://www.nfl.com/news/sam-darnold-silences-skeptics-as-seahawks-dominate-patriots-in-super-bowl-lx ; https://www.nfl.com/news/social-media-reacts-to-seahawks-win-over-patriots-in-super-bowl-lx

## 3) The 2026 NBA Finals

### Result and core facts

The **New York Knicks** beat the **San Antonio Spurs** **4-1** in the **2026 NBA Finals**. The series ran from **3 June to 13 June 2026**, and New York clinched the title in **Game 5**, winning **94-90** on the road in San Antonio.  
Sources: NBA Finals page: https://www.nba.com/playoffs/2026/nba-finals ; Game 5 box/recap: https://www.nba.com/game/nyk-vs-sas-0042500405 ; NBA Finals schedule page: https://www.nba.com/news/2026-nba-finals-schedule

### Clinching game

In the clincher, **Jalen Brunson** scored **45 points** as the Knicks closed out the series. NBA.com says he scored **15 points in the fourth quarter** and led New York to its first championship since 1973.  
Sources: https://www.nba.com/news/live-updates-2026-nba-finals-game-5-knicks-seek-first-title-since-1973 ; https://www.nba.com/news/4-takeaways-knicks-spurs-game-5

### Finals MVP

**Jalen Brunson** won the **Bill Russell NBA Finals MVP Award**. NBA.com called Game 5 one of the strongest closeout performances in recent Finals history.  
Source: https://www.nba.com/news/jalen-brunson-wins-bill-russell-trophy-as-2026-nba-finals-mvp

### Historical significance

This Finals carried several layers of historical weight:

- It gave the Knicks their **first championship in 53 years**, and their **first since 1973**.  
  Sources: https://www.nba.com/news/how-the-knicks-won-the-2026-nba-championship ; https://www.nba.com/news/2026-nba-finals-schedule

- It was the franchise’s **third NBA title** overall.  
  Source: NBA Finals overview: https://www.nba.com/playoffs/2026/nba-finals

- Game 4 became a signature moment: NBA.com says New York erased a **29-point deficit**, the **largest comeback in NBA Finals history**, on the way to a 107-106 win and a 3-1 series lead.

- Brunson, **Mikal Bridges**, and **Josh Hart** became the **first trio of teammates to win both an NCAA title and an NBA championship together**, according to NBA.com.  
  Source: https://www.nba.com/news/nova-knicks-title-new-york

## 5) NASA’s Artemis II

### Mission dates and basic arc

**Artemis II** launched from **Kennedy Space Center Launch Complex 39B** on **1 April 2026 at 6:35 p.m. EDT**. NASA says the mission safely ended with splashdown in the **Pacific Ocean** on **10 April 2026 at 8:07 p.m. EDT** off the coast of California, completing an approximately **10-day** lunar flyby mission.  
Sources: Launch day blog: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/01/live-artemis-ii-launch-day-updates ; splashdown live updates: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/10/artemis-ii-flight-day-10-re-entry-live-updates ; Artemis II mission FAQ: https://www.nasa.gov/missions/nasa-answers-your-most-pressing-artemis-ii-questions

### Crew

The crew was:
- **Reid Wiseman** (NASA), commander  
- **Victor Glover** (NASA), pilot  
- **Christina Koch** (NASA), mission specialist  
- **Jeremy Hansen** (Canadian Space Agency), mission specialist  

NASA’s official mission and crew pages list all four.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/mission/artemis-ii ; https://www.nasa.gov/feature/our-artemis-crew/

### Records and milestones

Artemis II delivered several firsts and records:

- It was the **first crewed Artemis mission** and the **first human lunar flyby since Apollo 17 in 1972**.  
  Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/mission/artemis-ii ; https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-welcomes-record-setting-artemis-ii-moonfarers-back-to-earth

- On **6 April 2026**, NASA says the crew **surpassed Apollo 13’s record** for the **farthest human spaceflight from Earth**. NASA’s flight-day updates give the mission’s maximum distance as **252,756 miles from Earth**.  
  Sources: NASA news release: https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasas-artemis-ii-crew-eclipses-record-for-farthest-human-spaceflight ; flight-day-6 updates: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/06/artemis-ii-flight-day-6-lunar-flyby-updates

- NASA also emphasized the mission’s public reach, saying agency-owned platforms drew **more than 149.4 million views** across launch, flyby, splashdown and continuous streams.  
  Source: https://www.nasa.gov/general/nasas-artemis-ii-breaks-agency-streaming-record

### Outcome

The outcome was unambiguously successful: NASA described the astronauts as returning safely after a **record-setting mission**, and agency updates said the flight validated spacecraft systems for future deep-space crewed missions, including later Artemis Moon landings.  
Sources: https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-welcomes-record-setting-artemis-ii-moonfarers-back-to-earth ; https://www.nasa.gov/mission/artemis-ii

For a newsroom, the clean takeaway is that Artemis II turned lunar exploration back into a live human story: first crew around the Moon in more than half a century, new distance record, safe return.

If you need one-line descriptors for the January–July 2026 almanac:

- **World Cup:** the first **48-team** men’s World Cup, co-hosted by **Canada, Mexico and the U.S.**, has already delivered knockout volatility, including **Paraguay over Germany** and **Morocco over the Netherlands**, with the **round of 16 now underway**.

- **Oscars:** **Conan O’Brien** hosted the **98th Academy Awards** on **15 March**; **_One Battle After Another_** won **Best Picture**, while **Michael B. Jordan** and **Jessie Buckley** took the lead acting Oscars.  
- **Space:** **Artemis II** launched **1 April**, splashed down **10 April**, carried **Wiseman, Glover, Koch and Hansen**, and set a **new record for farthest human spaceflight** before returning safely.

Short direct answer: The defining January–July 2026 stories were a history-making 48-team World Cup now in its round of 16, Seattle’s 29-13 Super Bowl win, New York’s first NBA title since 1973, an Oscars night led by _One Battle After Another_, and NASA’s successful, record-setting Artemis II lunar mission.

baseline@40.report:
# State of the stack, mid-2026
A CTO briefing on the AI + web engineering ecosystem

## Executive summary

As of early July 2026, the stack has clarified around a few strong patterns:

- **OpenAI’s API lineup** is now centered on the **GPT-5.x family**, with clear separation between flagship, mini, nano, and pro tiers, plus separate realtime, image, transcription, and specialized models. Pricing is explicitly split across **Standard / Batch / Flex / Priority**, with short- vs long-context pricing for top-tier text models. Official pricing currently lists **gpt-5.5** as the latest flagship model. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/pricing
- **LangChain 1.x** has completed its simplification: the modern default is **`create_agent`**, not legacy chains or the old ReAct prebuilt entrypoints. Legacy functionality was moved to **`langchain-classic`**. The production runtime under the hood is **LangGraph**, which is now explicitly framed as the durable orchestration/runtime layer for persistence, streaming, interrupts, and time travel. Sources: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1 , https://www.langchain.com/langgraph , https://pypi.org/project/langchain/
- **deepagents** has emerged as LangChain’s opinionated “agent harness” for long-running, multi-step work. Its center of gravity is **`create_deep_agent`**, with built-in planning, subagents, filesystem tooling, permissions, memory/skills, and human approvals. It runs on the **LangGraph runtime**. Current PyPI version surfaced in the official package page is **1.0.2**. Sources: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview , https://pypi.org/project/deepagents
- **Next.js** is now firmly in the **16.x** era. The current docs header shows **16.2.10** on the upgrade landing page and **16.2.9** on the v16 upgrade guide snapshot surfaced by search. The important architectural shift is not just “a new version”; it is the move to **Turbopack as default**, **Node 20.9+ minimum**, **explicit caching via Cache Components**, and **`proxy.ts` replacing `middleware.ts`** for the primary request boundary. Sources: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/getting-started/upgrading , https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16 , https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

The broad takeaway: **the ecosystem has moved from “LLM wrappers” and “framework experiments” into runtime-centric, production-oriented systems**. The winning patterns are explicit orchestration, explicit caching, typed tool surfaces, long-running execution, and structured human control.

# 2) LangChain 1.x: ecosystem status, modern API, legacy replacements, runtime layer

## 2.1 Current package status and versions

The official PyPI page currently shows:
- **`langchain` version 1.3.11**
- Python requirement: **>=3.10,<4.0**  
Source: https://pypi.org/project/langchain/

The same PyPI page describes LangChain as:
- “the easiest way to start building agents and applications powered by LLMs”
- built on top of LangGraph for “durable execution, streaming, human-in-the-loop, persistence, and more”  
Source: https://pypi.org/project/langchain/

## 2.2 What changed in LangChain v1

The official “What’s new in LangChain v1” page says v1 is centered on three changes:

1. **`create_agent`** is the new standard
2. **Standard content blocks**
3. **Simplified package/namespace**, with legacy functionality moved to **`langchain-classic`**  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

## 2.3 The modern agent-building API

The official recommendation is now explicit:

- **Use `langchain.agents.create_agent`**
- It replaces **`langgraph.prebuilt.create_react_agent`** as the standard entry point  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

Official wording:
> “`create_agent` is the standard way to build agents in LangChain 1.0” and provides a simpler interface than `langgraph.prebuilt.create_react_agent` while offering more customization through middleware.  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

That is the major ecosystem shift: **the new abstraction is no longer “pick a chain/prompt recipe”; it is “build an agent with middleware on a runtime.”**

## 2.4 What replaced the legacy patterns

### Replaced
- `langgraph.prebuilt.create_react_agent` as the default high-level entry point  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

### Moved out of the core package
Legacy functionality now lives in **`langchain-classic`**, including:
- legacy chains and chain implementations
- retrievers from the old module structure
- indexing API
- hub module
- `langchain-community` exports
- other deprecated functionality  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

So the 2026 message to engineering teams is simple:
- **Use `langchain` for modern agent apps**
- **Use `langchain-classic` only if you’re carrying old code forward**  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

## 2.5 Middleware is now the main extension model

LangChain v1 makes **middleware** the defining extension point for agents. The official docs list built-in middleware such as:
- `PIIMiddleware`
- `SummarizationMiddleware`
- `HumanInTheLoopMiddleware`  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

The docs also define middleware hook points:
- `before_agent`
- `before_model`
- `wrap_model_call`
- `wrap_tool_call`
- `after_model`
- `after_agent`  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

This is important strategically: LangChain has moved from “chains as composition units” to **middleware + agent loop as composition units**.

## 2.6 Standard content blocks

LangChain v1 introduced a unified `content_blocks` abstraction for:
- reasoning traces
- citations
- tool calls
- text
- other provider-native structured content  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

Supported integrations explicitly listed include:
- `langchain-anthropic`
- `langchain-aws`
- `langchain-openai`
- `langchain-google-genai`
- `langchain-ollama`  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

The architectural value is high: this is LangChain’s answer to provider fragmentation.

## 2.7 The runtime layer: LangGraph

LangChain’s own docs and site make the layering explicit:

- **LangChain** = high-level framework for building agents quickly
- **LangGraph** = low-level orchestration framework and runtime for reliable agents  
Sources: https://pypi.org/project/langchain/ , https://www.langchain.com/langgraph

The official LangGraph site describes it as:
- an **agent runtime**
- a **low-level orchestration framework**
- focused on reliability and low-level control  
Source: https://www.langchain.com/langgraph

LangChain’s v1 page says that because `create_agent` is built on LangGraph, you automatically get:
- **Persistence**
- **Streaming**
- **Human-in-the-loop**
- **Time travel**  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1

This is the runtime story in one sentence:  
**LangChain is now the ergonomic surface; LangGraph is the durable execution substrate.**

## 2.8 CTO interpretation

LangChain 1.x is no longer best understood as a “bag of prompt/chains utilities.” It is now:
- a **high-level agent framework**
- with **middleware-first customization**
- on top of a **durable runtime** (LangGraph)

For greenfield work, this is the right mental model:
- Start with **LangChain `create_agent`**
- Drop to **LangGraph** when you need custom control flow, deterministic branches, deep state control, or runtime-level observability

# 3) deepagents: current version and core APIs

## 3.1 Current version

The official PyPI page surfaced by search currently shows:
- **`deepagents` version 1.0.2**  
Source: https://pypi.org/project/deepagents

The official docs describe `deepagents` as a standalone library built on top of LangChain and using LangGraph as runtime.  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

## 3.2 What deepagents is

The official overview defines Deep Agents as:
- “the easiest way to start building agents and applications that are powered by LLMs”
- with built-in support for
  - task planning
  - file systems for context management
  - subagent spawning
  - long-term memory  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

It is explicitly called an **“agent harness.”**  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

That’s an important distinction:
- **LangChain** = framework primitives
- **LangGraph** = runtime
- **deepagents** = opinionated harness with batteries included

## 3.3 Core construction API

The central API is:
- **`create_deep_agent`**  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

The docs show examples using models like:
- `openai:gpt-5.5`
- `anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `google_genai:gemini-3.5-flash`
- other provider-prefixed model strings  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

## 3.4 Core capabilities / APIs to know

The official docs group Deep Agents into four major layers:

### A. Execution environment
Includes:
- tools
- virtual filesystem
- filesystem permissions
- code execution  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Filesystem tool surface
Built-in filesystem operations listed:
- `ls`
- `read_file`
- `write_file`
- `edit_file`
- `glob`
- `grep`
- `execute` (sandbox backends only)  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Backends
The virtual filesystem can be backed by:
- in-memory state
- local disk
- LangGraph store
- composite routing
- custom backend  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Permissions
The docs describe declarative permission rules via `permissions=` with:
- `operations`
- `paths`
- `mode`  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

### B. Context management
Includes:
- skills
- memory
- summarization/context offloading
- prompt caching  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Memory
Memory uses **`AGENTS.md`** files passed through the `memory` parameter.  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Skills
Skills use the Agent Skills standard and live in directories containing **`SKILL.md`**.  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

### C. Delegation
Two main built-in APIs:
- `write_todos` for structured task tracking
- `task` for ephemeral subagents  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Planning API
`write_todos` persists task state with statuses:
- `pending`
- `in_progress`
- `completed`  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

#### Subagents API
The `task` tool creates subagents with:
- fresh context
- autonomous execution
- single final handoff
- default `general-purpose` strategy or custom subagents  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

### D. Steering / human-in-the-loop
Human control is provided through LangGraph interrupts and enabled using:
- **`interrupt_on`** in `create_deep_agent`  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

The docs give `interrupt_on={"edit_file": True}` as an example.  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

## 3.5 Runtime layer and observability

The overview states directly:
- `deepagents` is built on **LangChain**
- uses the **LangGraph runtime** for
  - durable execution
  - streaming
  - human-in-the-loop
  - other runtime features  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

The docs also recommend **LangSmith** for tracing, debugging, and production deployment workflows.  
Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

## 3.6 CTO interpretation

deepagents is the strongest fit when you want:
- long-running, multi-step, file-aware agents
- a built-in planner/subagent model
- controllable approvals
- less custom orchestration code than raw LangGraph

It is not the lowest-level system; it is the **opinionated productivity layer** for complex task agents.

# 4) Next.js: current major version, runtime requirements, breaking changes, build tooling

## 4.1 Current major version

The official upgrade docs currently show:
- **Latest Version: 16.2.10** on the general upgrading page  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/getting-started/upgrading

The official v16 upgrade guide snapshot surfaced by search shows:
- **Latest Version: 16.2.9**  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/upgrading/version-16

The discrepancy is minor and likely reflects page update timing. The important point is clear: **Next.js 16 is the current major line.**

## 4.2 Runtime and platform requirements

The official Next.js 16 release notes list:
- **Node.js 20.9+**
- **TypeScript 5.1+**
- supported browsers: **Chrome 111+, Edge 111+, Firefox 111+, Safari 16.4+**  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

This is not a soft recommendation; it is a version-floor change.

## 4.3 The big architectural changes in v16

### A. Turbopack is now the default bundler
Official wording:
- Turbopack is now the **default bundler**
- stable for both development and production builds  
Sources: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16 , https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

The docs describe Turbopack as:
- an **incremental bundler**
- written in **Rust**
- built into Next.js  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

If you need webpack instead:
- `next dev --webpack`
- `next build --webpack`  
Sources: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16 , https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

### B. Cache Components replace implicit App Router caching
Next.js 16 introduces:
- `cacheComponents: true`
- `use cache`
- `cacheLife`
- `cacheTag`  
Sources: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16 , https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/cacheComponents

The official cacheComponents docs say:
- data fetching is **dynamic by default**
- you explicitly choose what to cache at the page/component/function level
- PPR behavior is effectively unified under Cache Components  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/cacheComponents

This is a major design change: **implicit caching assumptions are gone.**

### C. `proxy.ts` replaces `middleware.ts`
Official release notes:
- `proxy.ts` replaces `middleware.ts`
- `proxy.ts` runs on the **Node.js runtime**
- `middleware.ts` is still available for Edge cases but is deprecated  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

This is both a naming and runtime-boundary clarification.

## 4.4 Key breaking changes

From the official Next.js 16 release notes and upgrade guide:

### Version requirements
- Node 18 is no longer supported
- Node 20.9+ minimum
- TS 5.1+ minimum  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

### Removed / renamed config and features
- `experimental.dynamicIO` → **`cacheComponents`**
- `experimental.ppr` removed
- `experimental.turbopack` moved to top-level **`turbopack`**
- `next lint` command removed; linting no longer runs as part of `next build`
- `serverRuntimeConfig` / `publicRuntimeConfig` removed in favor of env vars
- AMP support removed  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

### Async request APIs
Breaking changes include async access requirements for:
- `params`
- `searchParams`
- `cookies()`
- `headers()`
- `draftMode()`  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

### Image behavior changes
Notable defaults/constraints changed for:
- `images.minimumCacheTTL`
- `images.imageSizes`
- `images.qualities`
- `images.maximumRedirects`
- `images.localPatterns` requirement for local src with query strings  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

### Parallel route requirement
- All parallel route slots now require explicit `default.js`, or builds fail  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

## 4.5 Build tooling status

### Turbopack support and constraints
Official docs note:
- Turbopack is the default
- Babel is automatically used if a Babel config file is detected in Next.js 16
- webpack plugins are **not supported** by Turbopack
- webpack loaders are supported/configurable
- some Sass features such as `sassOptions.functions` are not supported in Turbopack  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

### Platform support for Turbopack native bindings
Supported native bindings:
- macOS x64 / ARM64
- Windows x64 / ARM64
- Linux glibc x64 / ARM64
- Linux musl x64 / ARM64  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

On unsupported platforms, the docs say Next.js falls back to WASM bindings, which **do not support Turbopack**, so you should use webpack instead.  
Source: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack

### Build adapters API
Next.js 16 release notes describe a **Build Adapters API (alpha)** for custom deployment integrations.  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

### create-next-app defaults
The official release notes say `create-next-app` now defaults to:
- App Router
- TypeScript-first config
- Tailwind CSS
- ESLint  
Source: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16

## 4.6 CTO interpretation

For engineering orgs, Next.js 16 means:

- **Assume Turbopack first**
- **Audit custom webpack usage before upgrading**
- **Treat caching as explicit application design**, not framework magic
- **Plan a runtime policy** around Node-only `proxy.ts` vs remaining Edge usage
- **Budget for upgrade work** if you depend on old middleware semantics, old lint/build assumptions, or implicit App Router caching

# Strategic conclusions

## What has converged

Across OpenAI, LangChain/deepagents, and Next.js, the same macro-pattern appears:

1. **Explicit runtime layers**
   - OpenAI: model/tool/platform pricing is explicit
   - LangChain: LangGraph is explicit runtime
   - Next.js: Turbopack/runtime/caching boundaries are explicit

2. **Middleware / orchestration over ad hoc glue**
   - LangChain middleware
   - deepagents harness + permissions + interrupts
   - Next.js `proxy.ts` and cache APIs

3. **Long-running, tool-using systems are the default target**
   - OpenAI tool billing and reasoning modes
   - deepagents subagents/filesystem/planning
   - LangGraph persistence/streaming/human-in-the-loop

4. **Framework “magic” is being reduced**
   - Next.js moved away from implicit caching
   - LangChain moved away from sprawling legacy namespaces/chains
   - OpenAI pricing makes tradeoffs visible enough that routing is architectural

## What a practical 2026 stack looks like

For most CTOs, a pragmatic mid-2026 default stack would be:

- **Model platform:** OpenAI GPT-5.x, routed by workload
  - `gpt-5.5` for premium workflows
  - `gpt-5.4-mini` for high-volume tool-heavy work
  - specialized models for codex/research/realtime as needed  
  Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/pricing

- **Agent framework:** LangChain 1.x with `create_agent`
  - middleware for policy, summarization, approvals
  - LangGraph when workflow control gets complex  
  Sources: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/releases/langchain-v1 , https://www.langchain.com/langgraph

- **Long-task harness:** deepagents when you need task planning, subagents, filesystem workflows, and HITL out of the box  
  Source: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview

- **Web platform:** Next.js 16 with Turbopack-first assumptions and explicit Cache Components strategy  
  Sources: https://nextjs.org/blog/next-16 , https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/turbopack , https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/cacheComponents

## Main risks to watch

- **OpenAI cost sprawl** from tool calls, search, containers, and long-context output
- **LangChain migration drift** if teams keep mixing `langchain-classic` and v1 patterns
- **deepagents overuse** where a simple `create_agent` or custom LangGraph workflow would be cheaper/smaller
- **Next.js 16 surprises** if your app depends on webpack plugins, implicit caching, or old middleware assumptions
